steps.explanation: We begin with the left side and rewrite both terms in sine and cosine so they can be combined into one fraction.
Secant is one over cosine and tangent is sine over cosine, so both terms already share the denominator $\cos\theta$.
Adding the two fractions gives a single fraction with numerator $1 + \sin\theta$.

steps.description: Decide on a strategy to reach the target
steps.explanation: The right-hand side has $1 - \sin\theta$ below, so we aim to introduce that factor by multiplying cleverly by a form of $1$.

steps.description: Multiply top and bottom by the conjugate
steps.explanation: Multiplying by $\frac{1 - \sin\theta}{1 - \sin\theta}$, which equals $1$, is legitimate and will create a difference of squares on top.

steps.description: Expand the numerator as a difference of squares
steps.explanation: The product of a sum and difference collapses to $1 - \sin^2\theta$.
The numerator is exactly $\cos^2\theta$ by the Pythagorean identity, which is the key simplification.

steps.description: Write the fraction with the new numerator
steps.explanation: Substituting shows a $\cos^2\theta$ over a product that includes a single $\cos\theta$, ready to cancel.
Cancelling one cosine from top and bottom leaves exactly the right-hand side.
The left-hand side has been transformed into the right-hand side, completing the proof for all valid $\theta$.
